items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a study session on April 14 to discuss the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines planned infrastructure and development projects. This is worth following if you care about what physical improvements or construction projects are coming to Mountain View.

items.headline: City Council to Amend Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to its Residential Tenant Protections Program through a municipal code amendment. The specifics of what protections are being added or modified will be determined at the April 14 Housing and Human Services Commission meeting.

items.headline: City releases library facilities and customer experience improvement plan
items.summary: San José presented its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan status report on April 9, outlining improvements to library services and physical spaces across the system.

items.headline: Downtown building eligible for extra space in exchange for historic preservation
items.summary: The building at 405 Kipling Street can gain 5,000 square feet of additional floor area if it undergoes historic and seismic rehabilitation. This incentive program encourages property owners to preserve Palo Alto's historic buildings while allowing modest expansion.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space is under review. This represents a significant change to the area's character and housing supply.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing delayed, rescheduled for April 22
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was postponed from April 8 and will be rescheduled for a later date to be announced. Residents who wanted to weigh in on this project should watch for the new hearing notice.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully specified in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what construction project this involves and where it will take place in Los Gatos.

items.headline: City awarded $1M+ grant for Mexican Heritage Plaza arts school
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding will help expand arts programming and cultural education in the city.
